metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Major corrections from official rulebook, BGG FAQ, RulePop interactive rulebook.

key_rulings.text: When you Lead: deploy a card to a location (trigger its deploy ability), then collect the TOP card from a DIFFERENT location and gain that location's bonus — OR draw from the deck and roll the Rising die.
When you Scout: reveal the top card of the deck and place (NOT deploy — no ability triggers) it face-up on any location. Gain that location's bonus.
Deploy triggers the card's deploy ability. Place does NOT. Only the Lead action's Step 1 triggers deploy abilities. Scout and die effects use Place.
The Sovereign token is worth 10 VP at game end. Gained by collecting from Luna.
Each Helium token is worth 3 VP at game end.
Influence at The Institute: most tokens = 4 VP each, second most = 2 VP each, all others = 1 VP each.
At game end, lose 10 VP for each card in your hand beyond 7.
The game ends when any single player meets 2 of the 3 conditions, OR when any combination of 3 conditions are met across all players. Conditions: Fleet at 7+, Influence 7+, Helium 7+.
When you collect from Luna: gain/keep the Sovereign token, advance 1 on Fleet, gain 1 Helium, roll the Rising die, and trigger your House ability.
When collecting during Lead, take only the TOP card from the chosen location — not all cards.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **112 character cards** (65x100mm) — each belongs to one of **14 Colors** (castes) from the Red Rising universe. Every card shows: a core value (upper-left number), a deploy ability (middle text — triggers when you deploy the card), and an end-game bonus condition (bottom text — scores extra if you meet the condition at game end).
- **6 reference cards** summarizing turn actions and end-game triggers.
- **25 Automa cards** for solo play.

#### Board & Locations
- **1 game board** (410x284mm) showing 4 locations: **Jupiter**, **Mars**, **Luna**, and **The Institute**. Each has a card stack area and a bonus icon. The board also shows the **Fleet Track** (positions 0-14).

#### Tokens
- **60 Helium tokens** — red gem-shaped. The game's main resource. Worth **3 VP each** at game end.
- **60 Influence tokens** (10 per player in 6 colors) — placed at The Institute and scored via tiered majority at game end.
- **6 Fleet tokens** — one per player, placed on the Fleet Track.
- **1 Sovereign token** — a large ornate token worth **10 VP** at game end. It changes hands throughout the game as players collect from Luna.
- **1 Rising token** — crescent moon first-player marker.
- **1 custom Rising die** (22mm) — 6 faces: Jupiter (advance Fleet), Mars (gain Helium), Luna (gain Sovereign), Institute (place Influence), Banish (remove a card), Reveal/Place (deck to board).

#### House Tiles
- **6 House tiles** — each gives you an asymmetric starting power that triggers whenever you gain the Sovereign token:
  - **Apollo** — you take the first AND last turn of the game. When gaining Sovereign, reveal/place the top deck card.
  - **Ceres** — start with 6 cards instead of 5. When gaining Sovereign, banish 1 card from a location. Must banish 1 from hand before scoring.
  - **Diana** — when gaining Sovereign, place 1 Influence at The Institute.
  - **Jupiter** — when gaining Sovereign, advance 1 extra on the Fleet Track.
  - **Mars** — when gaining Sovereign, gain 1 extra Helium.
  - **Minerva** — when gaining Sovereign, roll the Rising die for an extra bonus.

#### Other
- **1 wolf-head card tray & lid** for the deck
- **1 scorepad** for end-game scoring
- **2 rulebooks** (multiplayer + solo)

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
Red Rising is a **hand-management combo game**. Your hand of cards at the end IS your score — every card has a core value plus conditional end-game bonuses. On each turn, you either **Lead** (deploy a card to a location, triggering its ability, then collect a different card and a location bonus) or **Scout** (reveal a deck card to a location and gain its bonus without giving up a card). The game ends when enough end-game conditions are triggered across players.

**Critical rule: you lose 10 VP for every card beyond 7 in your hand.** Your hand can grow, but excess cards are heavily penalized.

#### Setting Up
1. **Shuffle all 112 character cards** into a face-down deck. Place it in the card tray.
2. **Place 2 face-up cards** at each of the 4 locations (8 cards total on the board).
3. **Deal 5 cards** to each player as their starting hand (6 for House Ceres).
4. **Each player draws 1 random House tile** and takes their 10 matching Influence tokens.
5. **Place all Fleet tokens** on position 0 of the Fleet Track.
6. **Place the Helium supply** near the board.
7. **Place the Sovereign token** beside the board — unclaimed at start.
8. **Give the Rising token** to House Apollo (if in play) or choose randomly.
9. **Place the Rising die** near the board.

#### Checkpoint
You should have: 5 cards in hand (6 for Ceres), 10 Influence tokens in your personal supply, your Fleet token on 0, a House tile, and a reference card. The board shows 2 face-up cards at each of the 4 locations. The Sovereign token and Rising die sit nearby, unclaimed.

subtopics.title: Turn Actions: Lead & Scout
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
Do exactly one of two actions: **Lead** or **Scout**.

#### Action 1: Lead (Deploy, Then Collect)

**Step 1 — Deploy a card.** Choose 1 card from your hand and place it face-up **on top** of any of the 4 locations. That card's **deploy ability** (middle text) triggers now. Deploy abilities can include: drawing cards, gaining Helium, advancing Fleet, placing Influence, banishing cards, or other effects. You may deploy a card even if its ability conditions aren't met — the ability simply doesn't resolve.

**Step 2 — Collect.** Choose ONE of these options:
- **(a) Collect from a location:** Take the **top card** from a **different** location (NOT the one you just deployed to). Add it to your hand. Then gain that location's bonus.
- **(b) Draw from the deck:** Draw the top card into your hand. Then **roll the Rising die** and gain the result.

**Location bonuses (gained when you collect from that location):**
- **Jupiter** — advance your Fleet token **1 space**.
- **Mars** — gain **1 Helium** from the supply.
- **Luna** — the jackpot. You gain ALL of: the **Sovereign token** (take it, even from another player), **+1 Fleet**, **+1 Helium**, **roll the Rising die**, and **trigger your House tile ability**.
- **The Institute** — place **1 Influence token** from your supply onto The Institute.

#### Action 2: Scout
1. **Reveal** the top card of the deck.
2. **Place** it face-up on top of any of the 4 locations. (This is a PLACE, not a deploy — the card's deploy ability does **NOT** trigger.)
3. **Gain that location's bonus** (same bonuses listed above).

Scout is your move when your hand is already strong and you don't want to give up any card. You still advance on tracks and gain resources — without sacrificing hand quality.

#### Deploy vs. Place — The Critical Distinction
- **Deploy** = your card, from your hand, triggers its deploy ability.
- **Place** = a card goes to a location but its ability does NOT trigger.

Only the Lead action's Step 1 deploys. Scout and die effects always place.

subtopics.title: Colors, Die & Resources
subtopics.content:
#### The 14 Card Colors (Castes)
Organized by role in the Red Rising universe:

**High Colors (Ruling Class):**
- **Gold** — highest core values (30-40 range). Powerful end-game bonuses that reward building around them.
- **Silver** — Helium economy specialists. Cards that convert or multiply Helium.
- **White** — Sovereign token synergy. Cards that reward holding or gaining the Sovereign.
- **Copper** — Institute and Influence synergy. Cards that pay off when you dominate The Institute.

**Mid Colors (Professional Class):**
- **Blue** — Fleet advancement on deploy. Strong for Jupiter-track strategies.
- **Yellow** — Science and research abilities. Often cycle cards or reveal information.
- **Green** — Deck cycling (reveal/place cards from deck to board). Good for scouting.
- **Violet** — Creative and artistic themed abilities.
- **Orange** — **Wild for character names.** Can substitute for any named character in end-game bonus conditions.
- **Gray** — **Wild for colors.** Can substitute for any color in end-game bonus conditions.

**Low Colors (Laborer Class):**
- **Brown** — Move cards between locations. Versatile support.
- **Obsidian** — Combat and banishing abilities. Remove cards from play.
- **Pink** — Move cards between locations, similar to Brown.
- **Red** — Low core values but strong set-collection bonuses when you hold multiple Reds.

#### The Rising Die
Rolled when you draw from the deck during Lead, and whenever triggered by Luna or card effects. Six faces:
1. **Jupiter** — advance 1 on Fleet Track.
2. **Mars** — gain 1 Helium.
3. **Luna** — gain/keep Sovereign (triggers your House ability).
4. **Institute** — place 1 Influence token.
5. **Banish (X)** — remove the top card from any location entirely from the game.
6. **Reveal/Place** — reveal the top deck card and place it on any location.

#### Sovereign Token
The Sovereign is a single token worth **10 VP** at game end.
- You gain it by collecting from Luna, rolling Luna on the die, or through card abilities.
- When you gain the Sovereign (even if you already hold it), your **House tile ability** triggers.
- The Sovereign also acts as the **tiebreaker** — if scores are tied, the player holding it wins.

#### Fleet Track
Positions 0-14 with **progressive scoring** printed on the track (0 VP at position 0, scaling up to **43 VP** at position 14).
- Advance by collecting from Jupiter, deploying Blue cards, rolling Jupiter on the die, or collecting from Luna.
- Reaching position 7+ is one of the three end-game trigger conditions.

#### Influence Tokens
10 per player, placed at The Institute via location bonus, card abilities, or the die.
- **Tiered scoring at game end:** the player with the most Influence scores **4 VP per token**. Second most scores **2 VP per token**. Everyone else scores **1 VP per token**.
- Having 7+ Influence at The Institute is one of the three end-game trigger conditions.

#### Helium
Worth **3 VP each** at game end.
- Gained from Mars, Luna, card abilities, or the die.
- Some Silver cards can convert Helium into other benefits.
- Having 7+ Helium tokens is one of the three end-game trigger conditions.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Triggers
The game ends when **either** of these happens:
- **Any single player** meets **2 of the 3 conditions**, OR
- **Any combination of 3 conditions** are met across all players (by different players).

The three conditions (all at threshold **7**):
1. A player has **7+ Helium tokens**.
2. A player has **7+ Influence tokens** at The Institute.
3. A player's **Fleet token** has reached position **7+** on the Fleet Track.

Once triggered, **all players complete the current round** (equal turns). House Apollo takes the final turn of the game.

**There is NO deck-exhaustion trigger.** Only Helium, Influence, and Fleet.

#### Scoring Formula
After end-game abilities resolve:

1. **Card core values** — sum the upper-left number on every card in your hand.
2. **Card end-game bonuses** — evaluate each card's bottom text. Score bonuses for met conditions. Some cards penalize if conditions are NOT met.
3. **Fleet Track** — score VP based on your position (0 at position 0, up to 43 at position 14; progressive scale printed on the track).
4. **Helium** — **3 VP** per Helium token.
5. **Sovereign bonus** — **10 VP** if you hold the Sovereign token.
6. **Influence at The Institute** — tiered: most = **4 VP/token**, second most = **2 VP/token**, others = **1 VP/token**.
7. **Excess card penalty** — **-10 VP** for each card beyond 7 in your hand.

#### Tiebreaker
The player holding the Sovereign token wins ties.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Core Principle: Hand Quality Over Quantity
Every card scores its core value plus potential end-game bonuses. But at **-10 VP per card beyond 7**, a bloated hand is devastating. Aim for 5-7 high-synergy cards, not a pile of 10+ mediocre ones.

#### The Scoring Hierarchy
Know where the big points live:
- **Gold cards** have the highest core values (30-40 range). Even one Gold card anchors your entire score.
- **Fleet Track** can yield up to 43 VP — aggressive Jupiter/Blue strategies are very strong.
- **Sovereign token** is a flat 10 VP. Don't ignore it, especially if Luna is uncontested.
- **Helium** at 3 VP each adds up fast. 5 Helium = 15 VP.
- **Influence** is tiered — having the MOST tokens makes them worth 4x as much as the bottom tier.

#### Orange and Gray Are the Best Support Colors
- **Orange** cards act as **wild for character names** — they satisfy end-game bonus conditions that reference specific characters.
- **Gray** cards act as **wild for colors** — they satisfy conditions requiring specific colors.

Holding even one Orange or one Gray can unlock bonus conditions on multiple other cards in your hand. Prioritize them.

#### House-Color Synergies
- **House Jupiter + Blue cards** — Blues advance Fleet on deploy. Jupiter's House ability adds +1 Fleet when gaining Sovereign. Stack these for massive Fleet VP.
- **House Diana + Copper cards** — Diana places Influence when gaining Sovereign. Coppers synergize with The Institute. Aim for Influence majority (4 VP/token).
- **House Mars + Silver cards** — Mars gains extra Helium when gaining Sovereign. Silvers convert and multiply Helium. Stack Helium for 3 VP each.
- **House Apollo + White cards** — Apollo gets the first and last turns. Whites reward holding the Sovereign. Secure Sovereign early for 10 VP + White bonuses.

#### Lead vs. Scout
- **Lead** is usually stronger — you gain a location bonus AND a new card, at the cost of deploying one card.
- **Scout** shines when your hand is already strong (5-6 great cards) and you don't want to risk giving one up. You still get a location bonus without losing hand quality.
- **Scouting to Luna** is powerful — you get Sovereign + Fleet + Helium + die roll + House ability, all without losing a card.

#### Watching End-Game Triggers
- Track everyone's Fleet, Influence, and Helium. The game ends when 1 player hits 2 of 3 triggers, or 3 total triggers are met across all players.
- **Don't trigger the end before you're ready.** If your hand is weak, avoid pushing past 7 on any track.
- **If an opponent is close,** either race to finish your combos or deliberately slow their path by competing for the same resources.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Holding 8+ cards.** Each card beyond 7 costs you 10 VP. That's more than most cards are worth. Deploy your weakest cards aggressively to stay lean.
- **Deploying Gold cards.** A 35-point Gold deployed for a Mars bonus (3 VP in Helium) is a 32-VP loss. Always deploy your lowest-value cards.
- **Ignoring the Sovereign token.** 10 VP is massive. If nobody is contesting Luna, Scout there repeatedly.
- **Spreading across all 14 Colors.** Focus on 2-3 Colors that synergize with your House tile. Use Orange/Gray as wild support.
- **Forgetting deploy vs. place.** Scout and die effects PLACE cards — no deploy abilities trigger. Only the Lead action's Step 1 deploys.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Red Rising

**After Setup**: You have 5 cards in hand, 10 Influence tokens, Fleet at 0, a House tile, and no Helium. Each location has 2 face-up cards. The Sovereign token is unclaimed.

**Turn 1 — Lead**: Find your lowest-value card. Deploy it to a location — say Luna. Its deploy ability triggers (resolve it). Now collect from a different location — if Mars has a Gold card you want, take the top card from Mars into your hand and gain 1 Helium. You gave up a weak card and gained a strong one plus a resource.

**Turn 2 — Scout to Luna**: Your hand is looking good and you don't want to give up any card. Reveal the top deck card and place it on Luna (no deploy ability triggers). Now gain Luna's bonus: take the Sovereign token (10 VP!), +1 Fleet, +1 Helium, roll the Rising die, and trigger your House ability. All that without losing a card.

**Turns 3-5 — Build Combos**: Deploy weak cards, collect strong ones from locations. Watch for Orange and Gray cards — they act as wilds for your end-game bonus conditions. Keep your hand at or below 7 cards to avoid the -10 VP penalty.

**Mid-Game**: Check the board state. Which end-game conditions are approaching 7? If your Fleet is at 5, two more Jupiter collections trigger it. If your hand has strong combos, you WANT to trigger the end. If not, slow down and keep sculpting.

**Late Game — Optimize**: Stop deploying valuable cards. Scout if your hand is good. Make sure your end-game bonus conditions are met. Check: do your Orange/Gray wilds complete any conditions on your other cards?

**End-Game Trigger**: The game ends when 1 player hits 2 of 3 conditions (Fleet 7+, Influence 7+, Helium 7+), or 3 total conditions are met across all players. Everyone finishes the round.

**Scoring**: Use the scorepad. Add: card core values + end-game bonuses + Fleet Track VP + (Helium x 3) + Sovereign (10 VP if held) + Influence (tiered: 4/2/1 per token). Subtract: 10 per card beyond 7. First-game target: 80-120 VP is competitive.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Hand Sculpting
Red Rising is fundamentally about building the perfect 5-7 card hand. Every deploy-and-collect decision should ask: "Does this improve my end-game hand?" A powerful card on the board is only useful if you can collect it before someone else does or the game ends.

- **Deploy ceiling: never deploy a card worth more than the benefit you gain.** Deploying a 35-point Gold for a 3 VP Helium bonus is a 32-VP loss. Deploy your lowest-value cards.
- **Hand trimming matters.** At 8 cards, you're eating -10 VP. At 9, that's -20. Actively deploy down to 7 or below, even if it means deploying a decent card.

#### Color Synergy Stacking
- **Gold + Orange/Gray:** Gold cards have the highest core values (30-40) and demanding end-game conditions. Orange (wild for character names) and Gray (wild for colors) make those conditions dramatically easier to meet. One Gray card can unlock bonuses across multiple Gold cards.
- **Blue + Jupiter location:** Blue cards advance Fleet on deploy. Combine with Jupiter collections for rapid Fleet advancement. Fleet position 10+ is worth 25+ VP.
- **Copper + Institute majority:** Copper cards synergize with Influence. If you're placing 5-6 Influence tokens, you want majority (4 VP/token) — that's 20-24 VP from Influence alone versus 5-6 VP at the bottom tier.
- **Silver + Helium stacking:** Silver cards convert or multiply Helium. With House Mars (extra Helium when gaining Sovereign), you can stockpile 8-10 Helium for 24-30 VP.

#### The Sovereign Token Economy
The Sovereign token is worth 10 VP at game end, plus it triggers your House ability every time you gain it. This makes Luna the most valuable location in the game.

- **Collecting from Luna gives you everything:** Sovereign (10 VP), +1 Fleet, +1 Helium (3 VP), die roll, and your House ability. That's potentially 15+ VP from a single collection.
- **House abilities compound with Sovereign gains.** House Jupiter gets +1 Fleet each time. House Mars gets +1 Helium each time. House Diana places Influence each time. Gaining the Sovereign 3-4 times stacks these bonuses.
- **If nobody contests Luna, Scout there every turn.** You gain all Luna bonuses without losing a card.

#### Location Mastery
- **Jupiter** — advance Fleet. Best when you're running a Blue-heavy strategy or racing toward Fleet 7+ for the end-game trigger.
- **Mars** — gain Helium (3 VP each). Best for Silver strategies or when stacking toward Helium 7+.
- **Luna** — the full package. Best overall location but highly contested. Worth fighting for.
- **The Institute** — place Influence. Best for Copper strategies chasing majority scoring. Less impactful if you're behind in Influence.

#### End-Game Trigger Manipulation
The game ends when 1 player hits 2 of 3 conditions (Fleet 7+, Influence 7+, Helium 7+), or when 3 conditions are met across all players.

- **Trigger when ahead.** If your hand scores 120+ and opponents are still building, push past 7 on two tracks to force the end.
- **Stall when behind.** If your combos aren't complete, avoid advancing past 7 on any track. Let opponents trigger the end — you get to finish the round.
- **Watch the collective threshold.** Even if no single player has 2 conditions, the game ends when 3 are met by any combination. One player at Fleet 7, another at Helium 7, and a third at Influence 7 triggers it.

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2-3 players:** Locations stay stacked — more cards to choose from. Sovereign changes hands less often. Engine strategies have more time.
- **4-6 players:** Locations get stripped quickly. Luna is fiercely contested. End-game triggers fire faster. Prioritize hand quality early.
